items.title: Gaborone accuses Moscow of luring Batswana to battlefield
items.description: Botswana is the latest nation to accuse Moscow of using deceptive tactics to lure foreign recruits to the battlefield. Without saying how many of its citizens were involved, Gaborone's foreign ministry claimed its nationals were duped into going to Russia and then forced into active combat.

items.title: Map reveals Australia's 'pharmacy deserts' are not all in the bush

items.title: Israel and Lebanon trial 'safe zones' in southern Lebanon as part of deal
items.description: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun is in Washington to meet with US President Donald Trump in the first official visit by a Lebanese head of state since 2009. It comes at a moment of high stakes for the country. Israeli forces are now occupying a significant portion of southern Lebanon following rocket attacks by Hezbollah on northern Israel. Tel Aviv insists that the Iran-backed militia be disarmed before it withdraws, something the Lebanese army is likely unable to accomplish. As a result, Israel has launched a '"pilot programme'" for "safe zones", which effectively sees Israel hand control of three Lebanese villages to the Lebanese Army, as Beirut tries to assert its authority.

Zelensky faces deepening political crisis as protests dig in
items.description: In Ukraine, protesters say they’re giving Volodymyr Zelensky until Friday to re-instate the popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, and fire General Oleksandr Syrsky, the military chief of staff. The President removed Fedorov from his post last week, citing a clash between the two men. The protests have brought Syrsky’s unpopularity to the forefront. Through credited with stopping the Russian invaders at the gates of Kyiv in 2022, he’s since gained a reputation for brutality, and for wasting lives unnecessarily. He's also, as our correspondent Gulliver Cragg reports, accused of tolerance for corruption.

items.title: Joseph Aoun in Washington: Bilal Saab firmly believes 'this meeting couldn't have gone any better'
items.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Bilal Saab, Senior Managing Director of the Trends Research Group and a close friend of Lebanese President Joseph Aoun. Mr. Saab offers a cautiously optimistic assessment of a pivotal moment in Lebanese diplomacy, arguing that President Aoun's visit to Washington represents an important opportunity to consolidate international backing for Lebanon's sovereignty and state institutions. While acknowledging the formidable structural challenges posed by Hezbollah, regional tensions, and extremely complex Israeli-Lebanese security challenges, Mr. Saab contends that success will not come from military coercion, but rather a focus on the gradual strengthening of the Lebanese state. According to Mr. Saab, Lebanon is both a testing ground for American regional diplomacy and a fragile experiment in state-building that will require sustained political support and not symbolic declarations.

While two of the movement's members met with a government minister on July 20th, India's prime minister, Narendra Modi, is failing to address the issue for now. Delano D'Souza reports.

items.title: ‘I hear you’: Burnham’s pledge to ‘knock money off bills immediately’ signals politics of deliverism
items.description: Stuart Norval is pleased to welcome Matthew Torbitt, former Labour Party advisor, political commentator, and anti-homelessness campaigner. With only weeks to prepare for a long-term 10-year programme for government, Burnham appears to be prioritising measures that demonstrate immediate responsiveness to economic insecurity. For Torbitt, this reflects a politics of “deliverism” rooted in Burnham’s ability to communicate empathy and urgency. His appeal rests partly on an image of ordinariness, relatability and trustworthiness: a PM who appears to understand everyday hardship and who can translate that understanding into tangible action.

items.title: Why France's D-Day landing beaches could join Unesco's heritage site list
items.description: Normandy's D-Day landing beaches could soon be listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site -- marking the culmination of a project launched nearly 20 years ago. The UN's cultural agency is set to announce its decision between July 24 and 27.

items.title: Ukraine plunges into political crisis as anger over defence minister's dismissal grows
items.description: Street protests were held for a fifth day on July 19 in Ukraine, over the firing of Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov. Fedorov was dismissed following frequent clashes with top Army general Oleksandr Syrsky, sparking indignation particularly among young Ukrainians, who credit the tech-savvy 35-year old Fedorov with innovative drone tactics that have given Ukraine new battlefield momentum against the Russian invasion. Fedorov has publicly blamed the 60-year-old Syrsky for his ouster. President Volodymyr Zelensky is now seeking to defuse the mounting political crisis and restore unity to the country's military.

items.title: Unilever Nigeria raises interim dividend fourfold amid slight increase in half-year profit
items.description: As much as N11.5 billion, a 73.7 per cent equivalent of post-tax profit, will be allocated to equity investors for an accounting period in which the profit itself only grew by a single digit. The post Unilever Nigeria raises interim dividend fourfold amid slight increase in half-year profit appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.description: Argentina keeper says he is enjoying this World Cup campaign much more than in 2022, despite coming into the tournament injured. Leer más

items.title: Rubio says United States wants to globalise fight against far-left 'terrorism'
items.description: Argentina among nations represented at meeting on "The Resurgence of Political Terrorism" hosted by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio. Leer más

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: 'France wants to lead the way' with under-15s social media ban
items.description: French lawmakers are expected to pass a bill on July 21st banning social media access to children under 15. If approved, France would follow Australia's lead, where the world's first ban ​for under-16s ‌on platforms including Facebook, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube came into ⁠force in December, as concerns grow over the impact social media has on the health and safety of minors.

items.title: Andy Burnham becomes Britain's new Prime Minister
items.description: Andy Burnham is Britain's new prime minister. After meeting King Charles III at Buckingham Palace, Burnham officially took over from Keir Starmer, who stepped down after just two years in office.

items.title: West African leaders approve Nigeria-Morocco gas pipeline
items.description: West African leaders on Sunday signed an agreement approving the construction of a gas pipeline linking Nigeria to Morocco at a summit of regional bloc ECOWAS in Sierra Leone's capital Freetown.

items.title: Morocco prepares for 2030 FIFA World Cup with major hotel expansion
items.description: For the first time in its history, Morocco will co-host the tournament with Spain and Portugal. To make sure the event runs smoothly, the country plans to add 60,000 hotel beds by 2030, according to the tourism minister.

items.title: The war in Iran has left hundreds of U.S. diplomats in limbo
items.description: As the U.S-Iran war ramps up again, hundreds of diplomats are in limbo. The State Department drew down staffing due to Iran's attacks on Gulf states. It's unclear if they'll return to normal business.

items.title: Palestinians say Israeli forces kill two during settler attack
items.description: Violence in the Israeli-occupied West Bank has surged since the outbreak of the Gaza war, with rights groups and the UN reporting near-daily attacks by Israeli settlers

items.title: French parliament backs social media ban for under-15s in EU first
items.description: FRANCE 24's Clovis Casali reports from France's National Assembly, where lawmakers have approved a ban on social media for children and teenagers under-15, the first EU country to do so. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Philippe Coen, lawyer and founder of the NGO Respect Zone.

items.title: Yemen's Houthis announce 'maritime embargo' on Saudi Arabia
items.description: The Houthis say the embargo begins immediately and is a response to a Saudi blockade of ports and airports in Yemen.

items.title: Hasta el final: What propels Argentina's comeback kings

items.title: Travel quiz: join us for a trip around the world

items.title: Why are thousands protesting for education reform in India?
items.description: Thousands of mostly young people tried to march on India's parliament to demand changes to how the education system is run.

items.title: What's next for the Albiceleste after World Cup final defeat?
items.description: Argentina fell agonisingly short of winning a second consecutive World Cup title. As the team reaches the end of an era, speculation begins over what comes next. Leer más

items.title: Nigeria, Germany set to hold bilateral talks
items.description: The German minister is on his third trip to Africa this year. The post Nigeria, Germany set to hold bilateral talks appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Putin meets North Korean foreign minister in diplomatic push
items.description: The visit by North Korean Foreign Minister Choe Son Hui comes without an obvious occasion, such as a multilateral diplomatic event or state anniversary, and has raised speculation that she may be coordinating a possible visit to Russia by North Korean leader Kim Jong Un.

items.title: Gabon's President Oligui Ngema arrives in France for state visit
items.description: Gabon's President Brice Oligui Ngema kicked off a state visit to France on Monday, a follow up to French President Macron's visit to Libreville last year.

items.title: Burnham cabinet: Ed Miliband appointed U.K. Foreign Secretary, India-born Kanishka Narayan in charge of AI
items.description: Jonathan Reynolds was named ‌Secretary ⁠of State for ⁠Business, Innovation, Science and Trade in an expanded role ​after losing his ‌position as business Minister to Peter Kyle in a reshuffle under ‌former Prime Minister ​Keir Starmer

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: Israelis march to Gaza border demanding re-establishment of settlements
Ousted Turkey opposition leader  plans to create a new party
items.description: Turkey's main opposition CHP faces a potentially historic rupture after months of infighting over its leadership.

items.title: Extreme heat and weather conditions attributed to stagnant jet stream
items.description: It’s no coincidence that extreme heat is engulfing huge swaths of Asia, Europe and North America all at the same time.

items.title: Spain shatters Argentina's dream with 1-0 win in World Cup final
items.description: Disappointment for Argentina and skipper Lionel Messi in World Cup final; Barcelona striker Ferran Torres lashes home a thumping finish in the 106th minute to reward a dominant display and win Spain the World Cup. Leer más

items.title: Dozens feared drowned as boat capsizes in northern Nigeria
items.description: Rescue teams in northwestern Nigeria were searching Monday for survivors after a boat capsized with dozens of people on board.

items.title: 'Anti-ICE' army veteran started fire outside Manhattan federal building, say officials
items.description: Andrew Arrabaca, 43, was armed with two axes, a machete, three knives and fireworks, says the FBI.

items.title: From Maradona to Messi: Bangladesh's enduring love for Argentina
items.description: The craze for Argentina has endured for decades in Bangladesh, a country of 170 million people who have never qualified for the World Cup. Leer más

items.title: Watch: U.K’s new Prime Minister Andy Burnham arrives at 10 Downing Street
France confirms 27-year sentence for Rwandan ex-doctor Eugene Rwamucyo
items.description: Rwamucyo, 67, was again found guilty of complicity in genocide, complicity in crimes against humanity, and joining a criminal conspiracy to prepare those crimes. He was, however, cleared of genocide and crimes against humanity charges.

items.title: Ozempic maker sues US rival over ad campaign
items.description: Novo Nordisk has claimed that Eli Lilly is using bad data to compare the companies' weight-loss and diabetes products in its ads.

items.title: South Sudanese women say insecurity and lack of services threaten lives
items.description: Women in the remote village of Bangolo in South Sudan's Western Equatoria State told United Nations police officers that ongoing insecurity, a lack of healthcare and education, and limited access to basic services continue to threaten their lives and livelihoods.

items.title: Spain's World Cup champions return home for parade in front of almost 2 million
items.description: The players and their coaching staff met with the country's royals, and Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez. An estimated crowd of almost 2 million was on hand to greet the squad through the streets of Madrid.

items.title: Outrage in France as Macron appoints senator who fought gay marriage to key rights job
items.description: French lawmakers on Tuesday narrowly approved President Emmanuel Macron’s candidate to serve as Défenseur des droits (human rights ombdusman), despite vehement opposition from groups arguing that Senator François-Noël Buffet’s views run counter to the mission of defending the rights of the most vulnerable.

items.description: Thousands of fans brave cold and rain to give a rapturous welcome to Argentina's World Cup squad. Leer más

items.description: After Spain won the FIFA World Cup, the Spanish Football Federation posted a photo of the trophy celebrations, raising questions about whether Donald Trump had been edited out. We took a closer look.

items.title: Watch: Flooded towns and evacuations as Chile hit by heavy rain
items.description: A "state of catastrophe" has been declared in Coquimbo and Huasco following the downpour.

items.title: Philippines accuses Chinese coast guard of injuring Filipino sailor in disputed shoal
items.description: Chinese and Philippines authorities are blaming each other for a row on Monday between Chinese coast guard personnel and Philippine sailors in a disputed shoal in the South China Sea

items.title: Jordan faces growing risk of becoming latest frontline state in US-Iran conflict
items.description: As Iranian attacks intensify, Jordan is emerging as the latest frontline state in the widening Middle East conflict. Why is the Hashemite Kingdom a target for Tehran?

items.title: Russian attacks kill six in Kyiv, other cities; expose gaps in Ukraine’s air defences
items.description: Moscow has in recent weeks stepped up its use of ballistic missiles as it seeks to choke off Ukrainian strikes on oil facilities deep inside Russia that have caused critical fuel shortages affecting both the military and the civilian population.

items.title: Canada imposes temporary Ebola travel ban
items.description: Canada is tightening its borders over Ebola. Beginning July 20, foreign nationals who've been in the Democratic Republic of Congo within the past 21 days will be denied entry.

items.title: Master and apprentice as Spain, Argentina coaches meet in World Cup final

items.title: Spain: Emergency crews battle wildfire north of Madrid
items.description: More than 25 firefighting planes have been deployed to battle the blaze in central Spain, which has been raging for three days.

items.title: The 'precious' bond driving the Stingers to success
items.description: Australia's women's water polo team, the Stingers, were the most successful Australian team at the Paris Olympics, winning a stunning silver medal. This is the secret to their success.

items.title: China, Philippines clash in South China Sea ahead of ASEAN meeting
items.description: China and the Philippines traded accusations after a violent altercation between navy personnel in the disputed South China Sea. The incident heightened tensions ahead of a major diplomatic summit in Manila.

items.title: King Messi faces Prince Yamal in final with World Cup at stake
